resources.string: Operation cannot be executed in\nunsupported files.
Sets noise reduction processing for shots in which shutter speed will be 1 second or longer.
Turns on Long Exposure Noise Reduction. Processing takes as long as shooting.
Turns off Long Exposure Noise Reduction processing.
Selects the maximum sensitivity in ISO AUTO.
Selects the minimum sensitivity in ISO AUTO.
Sets the way in which images are grouped for playback.
Remaining card capacity may not\nlet you shoot the selected duration.\nCard space: %3d shots

Total number of content in this
application has reached limit.
Remove some content from this
application.
Shoots 2 seconds after pressing the shutter button.
Use this application with SteadyShot of lens off.
You can activate the shutter by positioning your fingers in front of the Eyepiece sensors. Eyepiece sensors are found around the viewfinder. When active, you cannot use the viewfinder.
You can activate the shutter by positioning your fingers in front of the Eyepiece sensors.
The shutter will not be activated even though you position your fingers in front of the Eyepiece sensors.
To use Touchless Shutter, make sure FINDER/MONITOR is set to Auto before launching this application.
Create Movie was canceled since an unsupported still image was included.
When the ISO sensitivity is \"ISO AUTO,\" the shutter speed in which the ISO starts to change is selected. Depending on the shooting condition, the ISO may not change following the set shutter speed.
The ISO sensitivity starts to change following a standard shutter speed.
The ISO sensitivity starts to change following a fast shutter speed.\nMinimizes camera shake or subject blurring.
The ISO sensitivity starts to change following an even faster shutter speed.\nFurther minimizes camera shake or subject blurring.
The ISO sensitivity starts to change following a slow shutter speed.\nShoots photos with low noise.
The ISO sensitivity starts to change following an even slower shutter speed.\nShoots photos with even lower noise.
The ISO sensitivity starts to change following the set shutter speed.
Cancel?\nSaves a movie(AVI) under creation in memory card.
Remaining card capacity may not\nlet you make a movie.\nCard space: %3d shots
An illegal still image detected.\nThe saving process has been canceled.
Function disabled.\nCannot rotate since the still image size becomes smaller than 1920x1080.
Insufficient battery may not\nlet you make a movie.
An illegal still image detected.\nThe previewing process has been canceled.
Sets whether to save only the effect image or the effect image and original images together.
Saves 2 original images and an effect image.
Restores all the setting values adjusted for the current theme to their defaults.
Restore all the setting values adjusted for the current theme to their defaults?
The exposure compensation dial is invalid on this application.\nSet the exposure compensation value with .
The aperture ring is invalid on this application.\nSet the aperture value with  when in exposure mode A or M.
Displays shooting tips for taking pictures with this application.

- Stabilize the device with a tripod when shooting.
- Set "Distortion Compensation" to "Off" before using this application.
The application is not recommended to use with lenses for which the compensation cannot be set to "Off".
- This guide can be seen from the "Shooting Tips" in the menu.
Shoots image with only different exposures.
- Stabilize the device with a tripod when shooting.\n- This guide can be seen from the \"Shooting Tips\" in the menu.
Performs settings of [Interval Shooting] such as [On]/[Off], [Interval], [Shots], [AE], etc.\n[Interval Shooting] automatically shoots in the set intervals.
Turns the interval shooting function off.
Sets the lower limit of the shutter speed to %s seconds.\nThe setting becomes available with shoot mode [P/A].
Sets the lower limit of the shutter speed to %s/%s seconds.\nThe setting becomes available with shoot mode [P/A].
Sets the lower limit of the shutter speed to %s.%s seconds.\nThe setting becomes available with shoot mode [P/A].
You can control exposure with a smartphone.\nScan the QR Code and access the URL.
You can set the lower limit of the shutter speed for the 3rd area.\nWhen the shoot mode is in [P/A], automatically controls the shutter speed so that it is not slower than the set value.
Displays only the still images shot with the interval shooting function on this application.\nDoes not display still images shot with [Interval Shooting] set to [Off].
You can set whether or not to establish Wi-Fi connection.\nSets to [On] when controlling exposure with a smartphone.
Sets the effect of the 3rd area.
Sets the position of the boundary for the 3rd area.
Sets the defocus condition of the boundary for the 3rd area.
Automatically shoots in the set intervals.
Does not set the minimum shutter speed.
You can shoot by setting 2 areas.\nYou can also set effects that seem as if graduated ND filters or graduated color filters have been used.
Will not be linked to settings of other areas.
You can control the exposure with a smartphone.\nScan the QR Code and establish Wi-Fi connection.
Copies all the setting values adjusted for the other themes to the current theme.
Sets the exposure by adjusting the aperture.
Sets the exposure by adjusting the shutter speed.
Sets whether or not to automatically rotate the boundary linked to the vertical/horizontal position.
Does not link to the vertical/horizontal position.
Automatically rotates the boundary linked to the vertical/horizontal position.
Saves the original images and an effect image.
After exposure, sets whether or not to adjust the boundary before recording the images.
After exposure, does not adjust the boundary before recording the images.
After exposure, adjusts the boundary while the shot image is being viewed before recording the images.
This setting is not supported by Angle Shift.
The still images with image sizes less than 1920 pixels are not compatible with Angle Shift.
The still images with this aspect ratio are not compatible with Angle Shift.
Limits the setting value of white balance.\nYou can swiftly set the white balance by limiting the function.
Does not limit the setting value of white balance.
Limits the setting value of white balance to color temperature/filter and AWB.
Limits the setting value of white balance to color temperature/filter.
Step 2: Adjust exposure and white balance.
Each theme maintains each camera settings.\nShoots with the desired camera settings by selecting the theme.
This function is disabled in this screen.
You can shoot images as if filters are used such as \"Graduated ND\", \"Reverse GND\", \"Graduated Color ND\", and \"Color Stripe\", etc., that are used to shoot landscapes.
Sets the position of the boundary between the 1st area and 2nd area.
Sets the defocus condition of the boundary between the 1st area and 2nd area.
You can layer the 3rd area effect over the 1st area and 2nd area. Adjust the boundary position and defocus while checking the 1st, 2nd, and 3rd area effects.

Assigns any settings among area settings for the first shooting in [Shooting Order], settings of the area last selected in the area settings screen, or settings of 3 areas (1st/2nd/3rd area) to the shooting screen.
Reflects the settings of the 1st area on the shooting screen.
Reflects the settings of the 2nd area on the shooting screen.
Reflects the settings of the 3rd area on the shooting screen.
Reflects the settings of the area for the first shooting in [Shooting Order] on the shooting screen.
Reflects the settings of the area last selected in the area settings screen on the shooting screen.
Shoots 3 times in the set order.\nWhen [Filter Sets] is set to [2 Areas Filter], shooting of invalid area will not be performed, and shoots 2 times in the order of valid areas.
Limits the setting value of white balance to color temperature and AWB.
Limits the setting value of white balance to color temperature.
You can set the lower limit of the shutter speed for the 1st area.\nWhen the shoot mode is in [P/A], automatically controls the shutter speed so that it is not slower than the set value.
You can set the lower limit of the shutter speed for the 2nd area.\nWhen the shoot mode is in [P/A], automatically controls the shutter speed so that it is not slower than the set value.
If you link the positions of boundaries, you can simultaneously rotate and move them up, down, left, or right.
When the position of boundary is rotated or moved up, down, left, or right, the position of boundary for other areas will simultaneously be rotated or moved.
Does not link the positions of boundaries.
The Digital Filter allows you to shoot images with effects as if the following filters are used.

You can use the Digital Filter for shooting shots of the land and sky with different brightness that were conventionally shot using filters or for shooting scenes you want to express different color tones of the land and sky.
The Digital Filter allows you to divide screens between 2 or 3 areas (1st area/2nd area/3rd area) and set exposure and white balance to each area to shoot.
The effect image is saved after 2 or 3 continuous shots depending on the number of areas.

For example, when shooting subjects such as the land and sky that have different brightness, you can create 1 image with different brightness of landscapes by setting the land as the 1st area with a bright exposure and the sky as the 2nd area with a dark exposure.\nFurthermore, setting the white balance of the sky allows you to adjust the color tone of the blue sky.

Furthermore, you can add the 3rd area by selecting [3 Areas Filter] in [Filter Sets] of the area settings screen.\nBy adding the 3rd area, you can set filter effects such as reverse GND and color stripe that are effective for shooting sunsets.
You can layer the 3rd area effect over the 1st area and 2nd area.
Next, the shooting flow is explained.
First, choose the composition of the image in the shooting screen.\nNext, press AREA SET. and proceed to the area settings screen.
Adjusts the boundary of area, and exposure and white balance of each area in the area settings screen.

Lastly, press the shutter button. The shutter will be released continuously, creating an image with the effects applied. You can also record effect images in RAW. To record in RAW, select it in [Quality] by the menu in the application.
- Reflects the setting of the 1st area to the exposure and white balance of the shooting screen. The area from where the setting is reflected can be changed in the [Shoot. Screen Set.].
- To move through the 1st area settings, 2nd area settings, and 3rd area settings, press the up/down buttons.
- To swiftly change the position of boundary, exposure, and white balance, turn the dial while placing the cursor on the items.
- To link the exposure and/or white balance with other areas, perform link settings by pressing the Fn button while placing the cursor on the items.
- To link and move 3rd area boundary with the movement of 1st area and 2nd area boundary positions, perform link settings by pressing the Fn button while placing the cursor on the item.
- To swiftly switch each link setting, press the  button while placing the cursor on the items.

- To swiftly change the color temperature of [C.Temp./Filter], set [White Balance Limit] to [Only Color Temp./Filter], and turn the dial while placing the cursor on the [White Balance] item. Recommended when using only [C.Temp./Filter].

- To swiftly change color temp. of [Color Temp.], set [White Balance Limit] to [Only Color Temp.], and turn dial while placing cursor on [White Balance] item. Recommended when using only [Color Temp.].
Adjust the boundaries of areas, and exposure and white balance of each area in the area settings screen.\nWhen you finish adjusting, press the shutter button to shoot.
Choose the composition of the image in the shooting screen.
Next, proceed to the area settings screen with AREA SET.
Adjusts the boundary of the image taken.

Links exposure&WB settings with selected area. When linked, setting value changes are simul. reflected on selected area. The setting value of first selected area will be used as default value of the linked settings.
Adjust the boundaries of areas, and exposure and white balance of each area.
Restore all the area boundary positions and boundary defocus settings for the current theme to their defaults?
Changed the white balance of the area set to [AWB] or [Underwater Auto] to [Flash]. When using flash with this application, white balance cannot be set to [AWB] or [Underwater Auto] with this device.
You can make link settings by pressing the Fn or  buttons while placing the cursor on the boundary settings, exposure and white balance settings.
Changed the white balance of the area set to [AWB] to [Flash]. When using flash with this application, white balance cannot be set to [AWB] with this device.
Copies all the setting values of the other themes to the current theme.\nSelect the theme of copy source.
Does not link the defocus condition of boundaries.
When the defocus of boundary is adjusted, the defocus of boundary for other areas will simultaneously be adjusted.
Sets the shooting order.\nWhen [Filter Sets] is set to [2 Areas Filter], shooting of the 3rd Area will not be performed.
To use [Touchless Shoot.], make sure [FINDER/MONITOR] is set to [Auto] before launching this application.
Sets the maximum and minimum sensitivity in ISO AUTO.
A setting sample with an effect like graduated ND filter. This default setting value is useful when adjusting the exposure of 2 areas with [Exposure Comp.] in Shoot Mode [A].
A setting sample with an effect like reverse graduated ND filter. This default setting value is useful when adjusting the exposure of 3 areas with [Exposure Comp.] in Shoot Mode [A].

A setting sample with an effect like layering Graduated Color ND filter (blue) over Graduated ND filter. Adjusts the exposure with [Exposure Comp.] in Shoot Mode [A] and adjusts the color of the sky with [Color Temp.] of [White Balance].

A set. sample w/ effect layering Graduated Color ND (amber) filter over Graduated ND. Adjusts the exp. w/ [Exposure Comp.] in Shoot Mode [A] and adjusts the color of sky with [Color Temp.] of [White Balance] or [Saturation] of [Creative Style].
Reduces the negative effects on the effect image occurred when shooting by including an overexposed part in the defocus of boundary.
Corrects color clipping and overexposure in the defocus of boundary on the effect image. This reduces the negative effects when the shot image includes an overexposed part in defocus of boundary.
You can shoot by setting 3 areas.\nYou can also set effects that seem as if reverse graduated ND filters or color stripe filters have been used.

A setting sample with an effect like color stripe filter. Uses 3 areas. This default setting value is useful when adjusting the color of center area to a different color while the areas above and below are the same with [White Balance].
[On]: Setting value is reflected to\nthe linked areas simultaneously.
